title: alnus rubra f. pinnatisecta
intro: Alnus rubra f. pinnatisecta, also known as the cut-leaf red alder, is a variety of the red alder tree, a deciduous tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The medicinal properties of Alnus rubra f. pinnatisecta include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of this plant are numerous, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Alnus rubra f. pinnatisecta is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, flu, and sore throats.
